Title: Add New Catalog
Preconditions: Admin logged in, Catalog module accessible
Steps:
1. Open Catalog module > Add > Click Catalog
2. Verify screen displays with required fields
3. Fill Catalog name field (required)
4. Configure Language block if needed
5. Click Create
Expected Result:
- System displays new catalog screen
- Catalog name field marked as required
- Language block visible and functional
- System saves new catalog successfully
- New catalog appears in catalog list

Title: Edit Existing Catalog
Preconditions: Admin logged in, at least one catalog exists
Steps:
1. Right-click on existing catalog > Manage
2. Verify edit screen displays
3. Review blocks: Name (required), Language, Property
4. Modify catalog name
5. Change language settings
6. Modify property settings
7. Click Save
Expected Result:
- Edit screen displays with catalog data
- Name field is required
- Language block shows current selection
- Property block displays correctly
- System saves all changes
- Updated catalog reflects changes

Title: Delete Catalog - Cancel
Preconditions: Admin logged in, multiple catalogs exist
Steps:
1. Right-click on any catalog > Delete
2. Verify confirmation dialog appears
3. Click Cancel
Expected Result:
- Confirmation dialog displays
- System does not delete catalog
- Catalog remains in list
- No data loss

Title: Delete Catalog - Confirm
Preconditions: Admin logged in, test catalog exists
Steps:
1. Right-click on test catalog > Delete
2. Verify confirmation dialog
3. Type 'Yes' in confirmation field
4. Click Confirm
Expected Result:
- Confirmation dialog requires typing 'Yes'
- System deletes catalog after confirmation
- Catalog removed from list
- Related data handled appropriately

Title: Add Catalog with Multiple Languages

Title: Create New Configurable Product in Admin Catalog
Preconditions: Admin SPA accessible at ${BACK_URL}. Logged in as ${ADMIN} with catalog edit permissions. Products with Options > Configurations category exists.
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} and log in as admin
2. Navigate to Catalog module
3. Navigate to the Products with Options category or equivalent test category
4. Click Add item or Add product
5. Select product type Configurable (or type that supports configurations)
6. Enter product name: QA Test Configurable Product
7. Enter product SKU: QA-CONFIG-TEST-001
8. Set product price to $100.00
9. Save the product (click Save or OK)
10. Verify product appears in category list
Expected Result:
- Admin SPA loads and dashboard is visible
- Catalog module opens
- Configurable product form displayed
- Name and SKU fields populated
- Product saved successfully with no error messages
- QA Test Configurable Product visible in category list

4. Enter section name: QA Test Options
5. Set the section as optional (not required)
6. Save the configuration section
7. Verify section appears in the product configurations list
Expected Result:
- Product detail blade opens
- Configurations section is visible
- Configuration section form opens
- Name field populated with QA Test Options
- Optional flag is set
- Section saved without error
- QA Test Options visible in configurations

Title: Add Option Products to Configuration Section
Preconditions: QA Test Options section created (CFG-CA-002). Existing simple products available in catalog to add as options.
Steps:
1. Open the QA Test Options configuration section
2. Click Add option or Add product to the section
3. Search for an existing product to add as option (e.g. Seat)
4. Select the product
5. Set the option quantity to 1
6. Set the option price or verify it inherits from product
7. Save the option
8. Add a second option product (e.g. Pedals) with qty 1
9. Save all changes
10. Verify both options appear in the section
Expected Result:
- Section management blade opens
- Product search/selector opens
- Product found in search results
- Product added as option
- Quantity and price set correctly
- First option saved without error
- Second option added successfully
- Both options visible in the configuration section

Title: Set and Update Option Price and Quantity
Preconditions: Options added to QA Test Options section (CFG-CA-003). Admin logged in.

2. Update the quantity to 3
3. Update the price to $25.00
4. Save the option
5. Verify the option now shows qty 3 and price $25.00
6. Verify extended price = $25.00 x 3 = $75.00 (if displayed in Admin)
Expected Result:
- Option edit form opens
- Quantity field updated to 3
- Price field updated to $25.00
- Saved without error
- Updated values displayed
- Extended price calculation is correct

Title: Mark Configuration Section as Required and Verify Storefront Enforcement
Preconditions: QA Test Configurable Product with configuration section exists. Admin logged in. Product published and accessible on storefront.
Steps:
1. Open the QA Test Options configuration section in Admin
2. Change the required flag to true/on
3. Save the section
4. Navigate to the configurable product on storefront
5. Observe the accordion label
6. Set quantity to 1 without selecting any option (with None selected)
7. Attempt to click Add to cart
8. Select an option from the section
9. Click Add to cart
Expected Result:
- Section edit opens
- Required flag enabled
- Saved without error
- Product PDP loads
- Accordion subtitle no longer says (optional) or shows (required)
- Quantity set to 1
- Validation error: Please select a required option or similar; item NOT added to cart
- Option selected
- Item successfully added to cart

- Price field updated to $30.00

Title: Publish and Activate Configurable Product — Verify Storefront Visibility
Preconditions: QA Test Configurable Product exists but is NOT published/inactive. Admin logged in.

4. Return to Admin, click Publish or change status to Active
5. Save the product
6. Navigate to storefront and search or browse for the product
7. Open the product PDP
Expected Result:
- Product opens in Admin
- Published = false or Status = Draft
- Product NOT visible on storefront (404 or not in catalog)
- Published flag enabled
- Product saved as published
- Product appears in catalog or accessible by direct URL
- Configure the parameters widget is visible on PDP
